{{Short description|Groups that advocate for child-adult sex}}
{{About|2=a list of pedophile advocacy groups|3=List of pedophile advocacy organizations}}
[[File:MAPs_Pride_Flag.svg|thumb|"[[Minor-attracted person|Minor Attracted Persons]]" [[pride flag]] used by some supporters of pedophilia<ref>{{Cite news |title=A flag for pedophiles? It exists, but it is not a push for inclusion in the LGBT community |url=https://factcheck.afp.com/pride-flag-pedophiles-someone-created-one-no-one-waves-it |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20260122091322/https://factcheck.afp.com/pride-flag-pedophiles-someone-created-one-no-one-waves-it |archive-date=2026-01-22 |access-date=2026-04-06 |work=Fact Check |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=LaMagdeleine |first=Izz Scott |date=2023-06-10 |title=Do 'Minor-Attracted Persons' Use a 'MAPs Pride Flag' To Represent Themselves? |url=https://www.snopes.com//articles/464807/minor-attracted-persons-flag/ |access-date=2026-04-06 |website=Snopes |language=en}}</ref>]]
'''Pedophile advocacy groups''' ([[American and British English spelling differences|alternatively]] spelled '''paedophile advocacy groups''') are organizations that advocate for lowering or alltogether abolishment of the age of consent and the normalization of [[Pedophilia|adult sexual relations with children]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=D’Ovidio |first=Rob |date=2009 |title=Adult-Child Sex Advocacy Websites as Social Learning Environments: A Content Analysis |url=http://ray.yorksj.ac.uk/id/eprint/3504/1/Adult-Child_Sex_Advocacy_Websites_as_Soc.pdf |journal=International Journal of Cyber Criminology |volume=3 |pages=421–440}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last=De Young |first=Mary |date=1988 |title=The indignant page: Techniques of neutralization in the publications of pedophile organizations |url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/0145213488900762 |journal=Child Abuse & Neglect |language=en |volume=12 |issue=4 |pages=583–591 |doi=10.1016/0145-2134(88)90076-2|pmid=3233524 |url-access=subscription }}</ref> Groups have existed dating back to 1962 in multiple countries including the [[United States]], the [[United Kingdom]], [[France]], [[Norway]], [[Germany]] and the [[Netherlands]].

In the 20th century, many pro-pedophile groups were founded during the [[Sexual revolution|Sexual Revolution]].<ref>{{Citation |last=Paternotte |first=David |title=Pedophilia, Homosexuality and Gay and Lesbian Activism |date=2014 |work=Sexual Revolutions |pages=264–278 |editor-last=Hekma |editor-first=Gert |url=http://link.springer.com/10.1057/9781137321466_16 |access-date=2024-02-16 |place=London |publisher=Palgrave Macmillan UK |language=en |doi=10.1057/9781137321466_16 |isbn=978-1-349-45804-2 |quote=As will be shown here, the era of sexual revolution was a very specific period for debates regarding pedophilia. Pedophiles made themselves publicly known and attempted to set up special-interest organisations. |editor2-last=Giami |editor2-first=Alain|url-access=subscription }}</ref> In the United States, pro-pedophile groups such as the [[René Guyon Society]] have existed since the 1960s. In Britain, the [[Paedophile Information Exchange]] (PIE), led by [[Tom O'Carroll]], has operated from 1974 to 1984 and openly collaborated with other political organizations such as the [[Albany Trust]] and the [[Liberty (advocacy group)|NCCL]]. In France, many intellectuals [[French petitions against age of consent laws|sought to lower the age of consent]] during the 1970s. The Netherlands was once seen as one of the main centers of pedophile activism.

== History ==

=== 20th century ===

==== United States ====
In the United States, pro-pedophile groups have often been inspired by their European counterparts, with some being transitory in nature and emerging in wake of criminal cases, and others being more long-lived. Some of these organizations have been investigated by law-enforcement agencies, with seizures of evidence, arrests and incarcerations of their leaders taking place. Some members of these groups have used pseudonyms to hide their identities.<ref name=":1" />
[[File:Rene Guyon.jpg|thumb|229x229px|Guyon in 1963]]
The [[René Guyon Society]] was the first pro-pedophile organization to be founded in the United States. The group was established in 1962 by seven people who had attended a conference by French jurist and pro-adult-child sex advocate [[René Guyon]] (1876–1963). During a 1977 subcommittee on select education, the organization's leader said that the group had 5,000 members nationwide. In 1971, the California-based Childhood Sensuality Circle was founded with the intent of advocating for sexual activities between adults and children. In 1978, the [[North American Man/Boy Love Association]] (NAMBLA) was founded following a police operation in [[Massachusetts]].<ref name=":1" />

==== Europe ====

===== United Kingdom =====
The Paedophile Action for Liberation (PAL) was the first pedophile advocacy group founded in Britain.<ref name=":3">{{Citation |last=Paternotte |first=David |title=Pedophilia, Homosexuality and Gay and Lesbian Activism |date=2014 |work=Sexual Revolutions |pages=264–278 |editor-last=Hekma |editor-first=Gert |url=http://link.springer.com/10.1057/9781137321466_16 |access-date=2024-02-11 |place=London |publisher=Palgrave Macmillan UK |language=en |doi=10.1057/9781137321466_16 |isbn=978-1-349-45804-2 |editor2-last=Giami |editor2-first=Alain|url-access=subscription }}</ref> Moreover, the British [[Paedophile Information Exchange|Pedophile Information Exchange]] (PIE) was established in 1974 and openly collaborated with political organizations such as the [[Albany Trust]] and the [[Liberty (advocacy group)|National Council for Civil Liberties]] (NCCL).<ref name=":1" /> PIE was affiliated with the NCCL from late 1970's to the early 1980s.<ref name=":7">{{Cite journal |last=Bingham |first=Adrian |date=2019 |title='It Would be Better for the Newspapers to Call a Spade a Spade': the British Press and Child Sexual Abuse, c. 1918–90 |url=https://academic.oup.com/hwj/article/doi/10.1093/hwj/dbz006/5374732 |journal=History Workshop Journal |volume=88 |pages=89–110|doi=10.1093/hwj/dbz006 |url-access=subscription }}</ref>

From August to September 1977, news articles were published on the daily denouncing PIE and its leader [[Tom O'Carroll]]. The organization remained notorious in the British press until the 1980s.<ref name=":7" /> In 1984, PIE was dissolved after its leaders were prosecuted for [[Criminal conspiracy#Conspiracy to corrupt public morals or to outrage public decency|conspiracy to corrupt public morals]].<ref name=":1" />

===== Netherlands =====
[[File:Edward Brongersma.jpg|left|thumb|185x185px|[[Labour Party (Netherlands)|PvdA]] Senator Brongersma in 1948]]
The Netherlands was once seen as one of the main centers of the pedophile movement. In 1971, the country had seen a federated network of pro-pedophile groups working in the [[Dutch Society for Sexual Reform]].<ref name=":2" /> During the 1960s, [[Frits Bernard]] and Dutch [[Labour Party (Netherlands)|Labour Party]] senator [[Edward Brongersma]] founded a pro-pedophile group.<ref name=":3" /><ref>{{Cite news |last=Pelgrim |first=Christiaan |date=2016-12-08 |title=Deze vier politici zijn eerder veroordeeld |url=https://www.nrc.nl/nieuws/2016/12/08/deze-vier-politici-zijn-eerder-veroordeeld-a1535645 |access-date=2024-02-15 |work=NRC |language=nl-NL}}</ref>

In 1982, [[Vereniging Martijn]] was founded and further sought to participate in political discussions on the topic of pedophilia.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-07-23 |title=Dutch paedophile club to fight their ban at the European Court of |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/europe/dutch-paedophile-club-to-fight-their-ban-at-the-european-court-of-human-rights-9622112.html |access-date=2024-02-11 |website=The Independent |language=en}}</ref>

===== Germany =====
In Germany, the German Study and Working Group on Pedophilia (DSAP) was highly active in the 1980s and had psychologist [[Helmut Kentler]] serving on its board in 1980. The DSAP also shared a post office box with the [[Humanist Union]], an organization that also had ties with advocates of adult-child sexual relations, in Düsseldorf.<ref>{{Cite news |last1=Gathmann |first1=Florian |last2=Müller |first2=Ann-Katrin |last3=Teevs |first3=Christian |date=2013-09-05 |title=Documents Reveal Links Between German FDP and Pedophilia Activists |url=https://www.spiegel.de/international/germany/documents-reveal-links-between-german-fdp-and-pedophilia-activists-a-920092.html |access-date=2024-02-11 |work=Der Spiegel |language=en |issn=2195-1349}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite journal |last=Paternotte |first=David |date=2014 |title=The International (Lesbian and) Gay Association and the question of pedophilia: Tracking the demise of gay liberation ideals |url=http://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/1363460713511103 |journal=Sexualities |language=en |volume=17 |issue=1–2 |pages=121–138 |doi=10.1177/1363460713511103 |issn=1363-4607|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

===== France =====
[[File:René Schérer and Guy Hocquenghem.jpg|thumb|Scherer (left) and Hocquenghem (right) in 1983]]
In 1977, many French intellectuals including [[René Schérer|René Scherer]], his former pupil and lover [[Guy Hocquenghem]] and [[Gabriel Matzneff]] launched campaigns [[French petitions against age of consent laws|to lower the age of consent]], which garnered the support of [[Michel Foucault]], [[Jean-Paul Sartre]], [[Gilles Deleuze]], [[Jacques Derrida]] and [[Félix Guattari]].<ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" /> Their writings were published in prominent French newspapers, such as [[Le Monde]] and [[Libération]]. During his career, Scherer wrote extensively on the topic of pedophilia from 1974 to 1979 and advocated for sexual relations between minors and across people of different generations.<ref name=":3" />

==== Among gay activists ====
According to researcher [[Theo Sandfort]], pedophiles had been seen as a sub-category of homosexuals until 1958. Attraction to boys was, to a degree, considered part of homosexual culture in Europe, and imagery associated with [[Pederasty in ancient Greece|Greek pederasty]] was common. This blurring of distinctions, Sansfort says, explains the absence of any specific pedophile group up to that point. Opposition to pro-pedophile organizations among homosexual circles has existed to varying degrees over time, with British, American and Scandinavian groups being more reluctant to support pedophile organizations than their German, Spanish, French and Latin American counterparts. In the 1970s, during [[Anita Bryant]]'s [[Save Our Children]] campaign, most American homosexual activists started to dissociate from pro-pedophile groups in the United States, which contributed to the subsequent creation of NAMBLA. The prosecution of PIE's leaders in the early 1980s also pushed some initially-friendly gay groups away from supporting the pro-pedophile organization.<ref name=":3" />

=== 21st century ===
[[File:BLogo.png|thumb|147x147px|The ''boylove'' logo, a symbol used by some pedophile groups<ref>{{Cite web |last=Holm-Nilsen |first=Sverre |date=2019-07-03 |title=Pedofilt miljø bruker hemmelige symboler for å kjenne hverandre igjen |url=https://www.nrk.no/norge/pedofilt-miljo-bruker-hemmelige-symboler-for-a-kjenne-hverandre-igjen-1.14613298 |access-date=2024-02-15 |website=NRK |language=nb-NO}}</ref>]]
With the development of the [[Internet]], many pedophile groups started to advocate for adult-child sex online.<ref name=":4">{{Cite journal |last1=Holt |first1=Thomas J. |last2=Blevins |first2=Kristie R. |last3=Burkert |first3=Natasha |date=2010 |title=Considering the Pedophile Subculture Online |url=http://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/1079063209344979 |journal=Sexual Abuse |language=en |volume=22 |issue=1 |pages=3–24 |doi=10.1177/1079063209344979 |pmid=20133959 |issn=1079-0632|url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{Citation |title=Adult–Child Sex Advocacy Websites as Learning Environments for Crime |date=2011-02-22 |work=Cyber Criminology |pages=139–162 |editor-last=Jaishankar |editor-first=K. |url=https://www.taylorfrancis.com/books/9781439829509/chapters/10.1201/b10718-13 |access-date=2024-02-11 |edition=0 |publisher=Routledge |language=en |doi=10.1201/b10718-13 |isbn=978-0-429-24638-8|url-access=subscription }}</ref> As of 2009, the Internet had 64 websites in 15 different languages that advocated for sexual activities between adults and children.<ref name=":0" />

Some traditional pedophile organizations, such as NAMBLA, have migrated to the Internet.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Quinn |first1=James F. |last2=Forsyth |first2=Craig J. |date=2013-07-01 |title=Red Light Districts on Blue Screens: A Typology for Understanding the Evolution of Deviant Communities on the Internet |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/01639625.2012.748629 |journal=Deviant Behavior |language=en |volume=34 |issue=7 |pages=579–585 |doi=10.1080/01639625.2012.748629 |issn=0163-9625|url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref name=":5">{{Cite journal |last1=Durkin |first1=Keith |last2=Forsyth |first2=Craig J. |last3=Quinn |first3=James F. |date=2006 |title=Pathological Internet Communities: A New Direction for Sexual Deviance Research in a Post Modern Era |url=http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/02732170600948857 |journal=Sociological Spectrum |language=en |volume=26 |issue=6 |pages=595–606 |doi=10.1080/02732170600948857 |issn=0273-2173|url-access=subscription }}</ref> The ''Free Spirits'' group, which has been funded by donations coming from its own members, has been one of the biggest known pedophile organizations online and has loaned websites to Dutch, German and Danish pedophile support groups, though they do not advocate for "physical contact" between adults and children. The Canada-based Ganymède group has also maintained similar websites.<ref>Tremblay, Pierre (2002). [https://epe.lac-bac.gc.ca/100/200/300/cicc/cahiers_recherche_criminologiques/CRC_2002_N36.pdf Social interactions among paedophiles]. ''Centre International de Criminologie Comparée''.</ref><ref name=":6">{{Cite journal |last=Campher |first=L. |date=2007 |title=A criminological overview of paedophilic activities on the Internet |url=https://journals.co.za/doi/abs/10.10520/EJC24318 |journal=Child Abuse Research in South Africa |volume=8 |issue=1}}</ref>
Pro-pedophile organizations have also produced online resources, such as the Wikipedia-inspired Girlwiki and Boywiki, that advocate for sex between adults and children.<ref name=":5" /><ref>{{Cite book |last=F Durkin |first=Keith |title=Extreme Deviance |publisher=Pine Forge Press |year=2008 |pages=144–150|url=https://archive.org/details/extremedeviance0000unse|isbn=9781412937221}}</ref> The ''Boylove Manifesto'', which has been shared by multiple online pedophile groups, states that sex between men and boys is not wrong and demands that age of consent laws be reconsidered.<ref name=":6" /><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Davidson |first1=Julia |last2=Gottschalk |first2=Petter |date=2011 |title=Characteristics of the Internet for criminal child sexual abuse by online groomers |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/1478601X.2011.544188 |journal=Criminal Justice Studies |language=en |volume=24 |issue=1 |pages=23–36 |doi=10.1080/1478601X.2011.544188 |issn=1478-601X|url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>Davidson, Julia (2009). [https://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/document?repid=rep1&type=pdf&doi=d2c02e7adec9aa7017b2bd9ea7ed91c791c580f3#page=39 Internet Child Abuse: Understanding Offender Online Grooming Behaviour]. Advances in the Analysis of Online Paedophile Activity. p 39.</ref>

== Characteristics ==
Pedophile advocacy groups often engage in [[Techniques of neutralization|neutralizing]] behaviors, such as ''denial of injury'', ''denial of victim'' and condemnation of those who oppose adult-child sexual acts. They engage in ''denial of victim'' by asserting that children are not victims of adult sexual behavior, but rather willing partners who are able to consent to sexual acts. This phenomenon is illustrated by a NAMBLA statement asserting that "if a child and adult want to have sex, they should be free to do so. Consent is the critical point... force and coercion are abhorrent to NAMBLA".<ref name=":1" /> Such beliefs contradict the academic literature that asserts that sexual behaviors between adults and children are harmful.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Durkin |first1=Keith F. |last2=Bryant |first2=Clifton D. |date=1999-03-01 |title=propagandizing pederasty: a thematic analysis of the on-line exculpatory accounts of unrepentant pedophiles |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/016396299266524 |journal=Deviant Behavior |language=en |volume=20 |issue=2 |pages=103–127 |doi=10.1080/016396299266524 |issn=0163-9625|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

These groups also engage in ''condemnation of the condemners'', a neutralizing behavior, by asserting that larger society is hypocritical and guilty of the same or even more victimizing acts that it accuses pedophiles of. This belief is illustrated by the following excerpt coming from their literature: "The 'protectors' of children are the real perverts, the real child abusers, the real molesters who take advantage of innocence and inexperience to spread the venom of guilt and fear."<ref name=":1" />

Aside from pre-Internet organizations such as the [[René Guyon Society]] and the Childhood Sensuality Circle, the use of neutralizing behaviors has also been observed in online pedophile advocacy environments.<ref name=":0" /> These groups often establish boundaries that separates their own subculture from general society.<ref name=":4" />

== See also ==
* [[Tom O'Carroll]]
* [[Ian Dunn (activist)|Ian Dunn]]
* [[David Thorstad]]
* [[Bill Andriette]]
